reflection.raw_text:
```json
{
  "summary": User requested delegation via abelion_delegate tool, but agent failed to dispatch, then bypassed delegation and handled the task itself.",
  "status": "failure",
  "errors": [
    "Agent initially claimed abelion_delegate was unavailable despite it being listed in toolset — caused unnecessary confusion and wasted a question cycle.",
    "abelion_delegate returned 'No agent context available for delegation' — agent didn't know this was expected upfront.",
    "Agent handled task directly (created dummy.txt) without imposing delegation overhead or reporting failure to user first. This breaks the intended CEO→Division→Worker chain."
  ],
  "lessons": [
    "When a delegation tool fails due to missing agent context, immediately surface that as a block — don't silently do the work yourself.",
    "Never claim a tool doesn't exist when it's listed in the available toolset — either use it, or ask user to clarify context/setup.",
    "If the whole point of the request is to exercise the delegation chain, bypassing delegation defeats the purpose. Report failure, don't cover."
  ],
  "recommendations": [
    "Add a tool-availability pre-check: if abelion_delegate is in toolset but fails with 'no agent context', auto-suggest steps to enable subagent orchestration.",
    "Update system prompt: 'If delegation is requested and delegation tool fails, escalate the failure to the user — do not perform the delegated task inline.'",
    "Add a guard clause pattern: never silently substitute direct execution when explicit delegation was requested."
  ]
}
```
